passages.text: The Industrial Revolution marked a major turning point in history. Almost every aspect of daily life was (8) --- in some way. In particular, average income and population began to exhibit unprecedented (9) --- growth. Some economists say that the major impact (10) --- that the standard of living for the general population began to increase consistently for the first time in history, (11) --- others say that it did not begin to meaningfully improve until the late 19th and 20th centuries. At the same time, the transition (12) --- a shift from hand production methods to machines.

The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 changed the course of medicine forever. Before antibiotics, even a small scratch could lead to a fatal infection. Fleming noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum had contaminated one of his Petri dishes and was killing the bacteria around it. However, it took several more years for researchers like Howard Florey and Ernst Chain to develop a way to mass-produce the drug. By World War II, penicillin was being used to save thousands of soldiers' lives.

Renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power, are becoming increasingly vital as the world faces the challenges of climate change. Unlike fossil fuels, which release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, renewable sources produce little to no greenhouse gases during operation. Despite their benefits, the transition to green energy is not without obstacles. The primary issue is intermittency; the sun does not always shine, and the wind does not always blow. Therefore, developing efficient battery storage systems is essential for a sustainable future.

questions.question: The sudden increase in oil prices has had a/an --- impact on the global economy, leading to higher transportation costs.

options.B: significant
options.C: artificial

questions.question: Eğitim, bir bireyin sadece bilgi düzeyini artırmakla kalmaz, aynı zamanda onun eleştirel düşünme yeteneğini de geliştirir.

options.A: Education increases the knowledge level of an individual and critical thinking.
options.B: Education not only increases an individual's knowledge level but also improves their critical thinking ability.

questions.question: Many historical artifacts were damaged during the war because they were not properly protected.

options.A: Savaş sırasında birçok tarihi eser, uygun şekilde korunmadığı için zarar gördü.
options.B: Tarihi eserlerin çoğu savaşta zarar gördü çünkü korunmaları imkansızdı.

questions.question: It is estimated that the world population will reach 9 billion by the year 2050.

options.A: 2050 yılına kadar dünya nüfusunun 9 milyara ulaşacağı tahmin ediliyor.
options.B: Dünya nüfusunun 2050'de 9 milyar olacağı kesinleşti.

options.E: 2050 yılında 9 milyar insanın dünyada yaşayacağı öngörülüyor.

questions.question: He was so tired that he fell asleep as soon as his head touched the pillow.

options.A: He fell asleep immediately after lying down because of his extreme exhaustion.
options.B: He was tired, yet it took him a while to fall asleep on the pillow.
options.C: Touching the pillow made him feel very tired and sleepy.
options.D: Even though he was tired, he couldn't sleep when he went to bed.
options.E: He went to sleep because he had nothing else to do.

questions.question: Hardly anyone expected the team to win the championship this year.

options.A: Everyone was sure the team would win the championship.
options.B: The team's championship win was a surprise to almost everyone.
options.C: The team worked hard to win the championship this year.
options.D: Few people were disappointed when the team won.
options.E: No one believed the team would even participate in the championship.

options.C: I knew you were here, but I forgot to invite you to dinner.
options.D: You should have told me you were in town for dinner.
options.E: Even if I had known you were here, I wouldn't have invited you.

options.D: The members were absent because the manager cancelled the meeting.

questions.question:
Amy: Did you hear that the library is closing for renovations?

Mark: I'm thinking of buying a new laptop for my graphic design work.

options.C: Laptops are very expensive these days.
options.D: I can lend you mine if you want.

Kevin: How was your trip to Italy?

questions.question: Tourism is a major source of income for many countries around the world. It creates jobs in hotels, restaurants, and transportation. ---. For instance, too many tourists can lead to environmental degradation and the loss of local culture.

options.A: However, it can also have some negative impacts on the local environment
options.B: Therefore, governments should encourage more people to travel
options.C: Tourism is expected to grow even more in the coming years
options.D: Most tourists prefer to visit historical sites and museums
options.E: Italy and France are the most visited countries in the world

questions.question: Sleep is essential for our physical and mental health. During sleep, our bodies repair tissues and our brains process information from the day. ---. Chronic sleep deprivation can lead to serious health problems such as heart disease and obesity.

options.A: Many people enjoy drinking coffee in the morning
options.B: Lack of sleep, on the other hand, can have detrimental effects
options.C: Dreams are a mysterious part of the sleeping process
options.D: Some people need more sleep than others to feel rested
options.E: Setting a regular sleep schedule is a good habit

options.B: Modern tourists love to hike along its various sections
options.C: The wall is visible from space according to some myths

questions.question: (I) Solar energy is one of the cleanest forms of energy available. (II) It is harvested using photovoltaic cells that convert sunlight into electricity. (III) Fossil fuels, however, contribute significantly to air pollution. (IV) These cells can be installed on rooftops or in large solar farms. (V) Once installed, they require very little maintenance and have no fuel costs.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: According to the passage, what did Alexander Fleming notice in his Petri dish?

options.A: Because Fleming didn't think the discovery was important.

options.C: Because the government banned the use of antibiotics.
options.D: Because soldiers did not want to use a new medicine.

options.B: It was discovered by accident in a lab.
options.C: It saved many lives during World War II by treating infections.
options.D: It is only effective against minor scratches.

options.B: Reducing the global consumption of electricity.
options.C: Developing efficient systems to store energy in batteries.
options.D: Relying only on solar power instead of wind.
options.E: Finding more fossil fuel reserves under the ocean.
